mission.title: Install and Configure Virtual Kubelet on Kubernetes
mission.description: Virtual Kubelet is an open source Kubernetes kubelet implementation that masquerades as a kubelet to connect Kubernetes to other APIs such as serverless container platforms (ACI, Fargate), IoT Edge, and more. It provides a pluggable provider architecture.

steps.title: Install the Virtual Kubelet binary
steps.description:
Build or install the Virtual Kubelet binary with your chosen provider. Virtual Kubelet is a library, so you typically install a provider-specific build.
```bash
# Install from source with a provider
go install github.com/virtual-kubelet/virtual-kubelet@v1.12.0
```

steps.title: Configure the provider
steps.description:
Create a configuration file for your chosen provider. Each provider has its own config format. Example for a mock provider:
```bash
cat <<EOF > vk-config.json
{
  "provider": "mock",
  "nodeName": "virtual-node",
  "operatingSystem": "Linux"
}
EOF
```

steps.title: Deploy Virtual Kubelet as a Deployment
steps.description:
Deploy Virtual Kubelet as a Kubernetes Deployment that registers a virtual node.
```bash
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: virtual-kubelet
  namespace: kube-system
spec:
  replicas: 1
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: virtual-kubelet
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: virtual-kubelet
    spec:
      serviceAccountName: virtual-kubelet
      containers:
      - name: virtual-kubelet
        image: virtual-kubelet/virtual-kubelet:v1.12.0
        args:
        - --provider
        - mock
        - --nodename
        - virtual-node
EOF
```

steps.title: Create RBAC for Virtual Kubelet
steps.description:
Create the necessary ServiceAccount and RBAC permissions.
```bash
kubectl create serviceaccount virtual-kubelet -n kube-system
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
kind: ClusterRoleBinding
metadata:
  name: virtual-kubelet
subjects:
- kind: ServiceAccount
  name: virtual-kubelet
  namespace: kube-system
roleRef:
  kind: ClusterRole
  name: cluster-admin
  apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
EOF
```

steps.title: Verify the virtual node
steps.description:
Check that the virtual node appears in the node list.
```bash
kubectl get nodes
kubectl describe node virtual-node
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show a new virtual node registered in the cluster. Pods can be scheduled to this virtual node using taints, tolerations, and node selectors, and will be handled by the configured provider backend.

uninstall.title: Remove the Virtual Kubelet deployment
uninstall.description: Delete the Virtual Kubelet Deployment and associated RBAC resources.

uninstall.title: Drain and remove the virtual node
uninstall.description: Drain pods from the virtual node and delete the node resource.

upgrade.title: Update the container image
upgrade.description: Update the Virtual Kubelet Deployment to use the latest image version.

troubleshooting.title: Virtual node not appearing
troubleshooting.description:
Check the Virtual Kubelet pod logs for registration errors.
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=virtual-kubelet -n kube-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in pending on virtual node
troubleshooting.description: Verify the provider is correctly configured and the backend service is reachable.

troubleshooting.title: RBAC permission errors
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure the ServiceAccount has sufficient permissions to register nodes and manage pods.
```bash
kubectl auth can-i create nodes --as=system:serviceaccount:kube-system:virtual-kubelet
```

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher with kubectl installed. A provider backend (e.g., Azure ACI, AWS Fargate) is needed for production use.
